Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.content: LEFT also changed 109 cells. Let me check what moved. I see the shapes appear shifted left visually. Let me check the exact positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After LEFT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        print(f"  {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  White at: {n['boundary']}")

# Compare with original to see shift
print("\nOriginal positions:")
print("  Black shape: rows 15-23, cols 18-26")
print("  Gray shape: rows 15-23, cols 36-44")
print("  Yellow shape: rows 45-53, cols 51-59")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After LEFT:
- Black shape: cols 15-23 (was 18-26, shifted left by 3)
- Gray shape: cols 39-47 (was 36-44, shifted RIGHT by 3!)
- Yellow shape: unchanged at cols 51-59
So LEFT moves the black shape left and the gray shape right (they move in opposite directions horizontally). Similarly, UP/DOWN probably move them in opposite directions vertically.
Wait, let me reconsider. Looking at the white dots:
- Before: (16,19), (16,22), (16,25), (19,25), (22,25)
- After LEFT: (16,16), (16,19), (16,22), (19,22), (22,22)
The black shape moved left by 3 columns (19→16, 22→19, 25→22).
Gray shape: cols 36-44 → 39-47 (moved right by 3).
So LEFT/RIGHT move the two shapes in opposite horizontal directions, and UP/DOWN move them in opposite vertical directions.
The goal is likely to align the two shapes somehow, or to match the white dot pattern with something. Let me think about what the goal could be.
Actually, looking more carefully at the shapes:
- Left (black): "F" shape with white dots
- Right (dark gray): "L" shape (horizontal bar on top, vertical bar going down on left)
The white dots on the black shape form a pattern. Maybe I need to move the shapes so they overlap or align in some way.
Let me try RIGHT to see if it reverses LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# LEFT moves black shape left, gray shape right (opposite directions)
# Let me try RIGHT to confirm it reverses
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
